titleStmt.title: A praise poem of Ḫammu-rābi (Ḫammu-rābi A) -- an English prose translation

publicationStmt.distributor: The Electronic Text Corpus of Sumerian Literature

address.addrLine: The Oriental Institute, University of Oxford, Pusey Lane, Oxford OX1 2LE, UK, email etcsl@orinst.ox.ac.uk, http://etcsl.orinst.ox.ac.uk

seriesStmt.p: The Electronic Text Corpus of Sumerian Literature aims to make accessible over 400 literary works composed in the Sumerian language in ancient Mesopotamia during the late third and early second millennia BC. The works are in Sumerian composite transliteration and English prose translation with bibliographical information on each composition.

sourceDesc.p: Sources are listed in the header of the composite text

div1.p:
…… acting as its lord ……
…… the black-headed ……. …… the Euphrates ……. …… the Tigris ……. …… on the banks of the Irnina watercourse ……. King Ḫammu-rābi …… Gibil the god of fire. Enlil ……. Enki …… heroism. Suen …….

Utu the sorcerer ……
…… his favourite ……. Iškur …… heroism. Marduk …… strength (?). Inana …… divine powers. …… reverent ……

…… humanity …….
…… the Great Mountain …….
…… the divine powers of kingship …….
…… Enlil …….
…… acting as its lord (?).

…… Enki ……. …… emerging from the Land. …… befitting divinity …….
…… joyous ……. …… praising with their songs ……. …… your statue shall not be brought in ……
…… formed your heart for Enlil (?). …… enter ……. …… precious destinies …….

…… its prince (?) Marduk who ……. …… curse ……. …… great wrath …… Marduk ……. …… midst of the great gods ……
…… weapon …….
…… brickwork (?) …….
…… portion …….
…… the black-headed …….
